title: [InstCombine] Narrow the ctpop when upper bits are known zero
author: RutanshS

tests.test_body:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare <2 x i32> @llvm.ctpop.v2i32(<2 x i32>) #0

define <2 x i32> @ctpop_add_no_common_bits_vec_use2(<2 x i32> %a, <2 x i32> %b, ptr %p) {
  %shl16 = shl <2 x i32> %a, splat (i32 16)
  %ctpop1 = tail call <2 x i32> @llvm.ctpop.v2i32(<2 x i32> %shl16)
  store <2 x i32> %ctpop1, ptr %p, align 8
  %lshl16 = lshr <2 x i32> %b, splat (i32 16)
  %ctpop2 = tail call <2 x i32> @llvm.ctpop.v2i32(<2 x i32> %lshl16)
  %res = add <2 x i32> %ctpop1, %ctpop2
  ret <2 x i32> %res
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

; Function Attrs: nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare <2 x i32> @llvm.ctpop.v2i32(<2 x i32>) #0

define <2 x i32> @ctpop_add_no_common_bits_vec_use(<2 x i32> %a, <2 x i32> %b, ptr %p) {
  %shl16 = shl <2 x i32> %a, splat (i32 16)
  %ctpop1 = tail call <2 x i32> @llvm.ctpop.v2i32(<2 x i32> %shl16)
  %lshl16 = lshr <2 x i32> %b, splat (i32 16)
  %ctpop2 = tail call <2 x i32> @llvm.ctpop.v2i32(<2 x i32> %lshl16)
  store <2 x i32> %ctpop2, ptr %p, align 8
  %res = add <2 x i32> %ctpop1, %ctpop2
  ret <2 x i32> %res
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

; Function Attrs: nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32) #0

define i32 @ctpop_add(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
  %and8 = and i32 %a, 8
  %ctpop1 = tail call i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32 %and8)
  %and2 = and i32 %b, 2
  %ctpop2 = tail call i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32 %and2)
  %res = add i32 %ctpop1, %ctpop2
  ret i32 %res
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

; Function Attrs: nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32) #0

define i32 @ctpop_add_no_common_bits(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
  %shl16 = shl i32 %a, 16
  %ctpop1 = tail call i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32 %shl16)
  %lshl16 = lshr i32 %b, 16
  %ctpop2 = tail call i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32 %lshl16)
  %res = add i32 %ctpop1, %ctpop2
  ret i32 %res
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

; Function Attrs: nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare <2 x i32> @llvm.ctpop.v2i32(<2 x i32>) #0

define <2 x i32> @ctpop_add_no_common_bits_vec(<2 x i32> %a, <2 x i32> %b) {
  %shl16 = shl <2 x i32> %a, splat (i32 16)
  %ctpop1 = tail call <2 x i32> @llvm.ctpop.v2i32(<2 x i32> %shl16)
  %lshl16 = lshr <2 x i32> %b, splat (i32 16)
  %ctpop2 = tail call <2 x i32> @llvm.ctpop.v2i32(<2 x i32> %lshl16)
  %res = add <2 x i32> %ctpop1, %ctpop2
  ret <2 x i32> %res
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

define i1 @isnot_pow2or0_negate_op_commute2(i32 %p) {
  %x = urem i32 42, %p
  %neg = sub i32 0, %x
  %and = and i32 %neg, %x
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %x, %and
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @is_pow2or0_negate_op_commute1(i32 %p) {
  %x = srem i32 42, %p
  %neg = sub i32 0, %x
  %and = and i32 %x, %neg
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %and, %x
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @isnot_pow2or0_negate_op_commute3(i32 %p) {
  %x = urem i32 42, %p
  %neg = sub i32 0, %x
  %and = and i32 %x, %neg
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %x, %and
  ret i1 %cmp
}

comments.author: llvmorg-github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM PR SUMMARY COMMENT-->

@llvm/pr-subscribers-llvm-transforms

Author: Rutansh Suthar (RutanshS)

<details>
<summary>Changes</summary>

Fixes #<!-- -->196434

---
Full diff: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/196537.diff


2 Files Affected:

- (modified) llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp (+12) 
- (modified) llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/ctpop.ll (+39) 


``````````diff
diff --git a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp
index da258b3b34e27..9968d2d9371d7 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp
@@ -696,6 +696,18 @@ static Instruction *foldCtpop(IntrinsicInst &II, InstCombinerImpl &IC) {
   KnownBits Known(BitWidth);
   IC.computeKnownBits(Op0, Known, &II);
 
+  // If the upper half of the bits are known zero, narrow:
+  // ctpop(x) --> zext(ctpop(trunc(x)))
+  if (BitWidth >= 64) {
+    unsigned HalfWidth = BitWidth / 2;
+    if (Known.countMinLeadingZeros() >= HalfWidth) {
+      Type *NarrowTy = IntegerType::get(II.getContext(), HalfWidth);
+      Value *Trunc = IC.Builder.CreateTrunc(Op0, NarrowTy);
+      Value *NarrowPop =
+          IC.Builder.CreateUnaryIntrinsic(Intrinsic::ctpop, Trunc);
+      return CastInst::Create(Instruction::ZExt, NarrowPop, Ty);
+    }
+  }
   // If all bits are zero except for exactly one fixed bit, then the result
   // must be 0 or 1, and we can get that answer by shifting to LSB:
   // ctpop (X & 32) --> (X & 32) >> 5
diff --git a/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/ctpop.ll b/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/ctpop.ll
index 217a3c308a2dd..f96cd1f96eea7 100644
--- a/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/ctpop.ll
+++ b/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/ctpop.ll
@@ -503,3 +503,42 @@ define i32 @ctpop_non_zero_with_existing_range_attr(i32 range(i32 1, 255) %x) {
   %ctpop = call range(i32 0, 9) i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32 %x)
   ret i32 %ctpop
 }
+
+define i32 @ctpop_narrow_i64_to_i32_via_assume(i64 %x) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: @ctpop_narrow_i64_to_i32_via_assume(
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = icmp ult i64 [[X:%.*]], 4294967296
+; CHECK-NEXT:    call void @llvm.assume(i1 [[CMP]])
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP1:%.*]] = trunc nuw i64 [[X]] to i32
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP2:%.*]] = call range(i32 0, 33) i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32 [[TMP1]])
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 [[TMP2]]
+;
+  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %x, 4294967296
+  call void @llvm.assume(i1 %cmp)
+  %pop = call i64 @llvm.ctpop.i64(i64 %x)
+  %trunc = trunc i64 %pop to i32
+  ret i32 %trunc
+}
+
+define i64 @ctpop_no_narrow_no_assume(i64 %x) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: @ctpop_no_narrow_no_assume(
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[POP:%.*]] = call range(i64 0, 65) i64 @llvm.ctpop.i64(i64 [[X:%.*]])
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i64 [[POP]]
+;
+  %pop = call i64 @llvm.ctpop.i64(i64 %x)
+  ret i64 %pop
+}
+
+define i32 @ctpop_no_narrow_not_enough_zeros(i64 %x) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: @ctpop_no_narrow_not_enough_zeros(
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = icmp ult i64 [[X:%.*]], 8589934592
+; CHECK-NEXT:    call void @llvm.assume(i1 [[CMP]])
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[POP:%.*]] = call range(i64 0, 34) i64 @llvm.ctpop.i64(i64 [[X]])
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TRUNC:%.*]] = trunc nuw nsw i64 [[POP]] to i32
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 [[TRUNC]]
+;
+  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %x, 8589934592
+  call void @llvm.assume(i1 %cmp)
+  %pop = call i64 @llvm.ctpop.i64(i64 %x)
+  %trunc = trunc i64 %pop to i32
+  ret i32 %trunc
+}

``````````

</details>

This comment is generated by an automated correctness checking service designed to help identify critical correctness bugs (opt crashes or Alive2 miscompilations) and improve PR review efficiency under limited reviewer bandwidth.

## Status: Previously reported issue no longer reproduces

The correctness bug previously reported by [llvm-hackme](https://github.com/dtcxzyw/llvm-hackme) no longer reproduces with the current PR update, and fuzzing did not identify any new correctness bugs (opt crashes or Alive2 miscompilations).

comments.author: RutanshS
comments.body:
> Do we not get any kind of ISD::AssertZext node when moving to DAG?

For test cases like func6 we don't get an ISD::AssertZext node because the argument is a 64 bit int passed into a 64 bit register, so SelectionDAGBuilder has no reason to emit an AssertZext.

The only reason we know that the upper bits are zero is because of the _builtin_unreachable() which translates to a llvm.assume. And because they are dropped before lowering to DAG, the backend loses this information.

comments.author: RutanshS
comments.body: Addressed review comments. Removed magic numbers, now narrows to bit_ceil(ActiveBits) which handles non-pow2 types like i65 correctly. Added i65 regression test. I skipped a minimum width to keep it target independent.
